title: Podman doesn't fail when joining a custom CNI network with rootless container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo podman network create customnet`

2. 
```
podman run -it --rm --network customnet alpine ip a
1: lo: <LOOPBACK> mtu 65536 qdisc noop state DOWN qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
```

3.
```
sudo podman run -it --rm --network customnet alpine ip a
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
3: eth0@if66: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP 
    link/ether 0a:70:82:4d:6b:2a brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.89.2.2/24 brd 10.89.2.255 scope global eth0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::870:82ff:fe4d:6b2a/64 scope link tentative 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
```

**Describe the results you received:**
When running rootless podman and attaching to custom network created by `podman network` it silently ignores network and runs without warning/error, just doesn't have network interface inside.

**Describe the results you expected:**
If it's not allowed to attach to network, container run should fail with an appropriate message, 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Actually as workaround for rootless containers to run with custom IP range could be changing the default CNI network range.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 
Version:            1.6.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.9
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

host:
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Why that ?

I can write a c application that creates a passive endpoint for portnumber > 1024 (or more?) using the IP-device ? So why do you forbid that ?
If it would work, then we should support it. But the rootless user would need to be able to hook up the network,  I would worry about UserNamespace and potentially SELinux blocking this access.

Sorry, my fault. I have overseen den port-mapper option in the manual:
`podman run  -ti -d  -p 192.168.178.39:9190:80`
that provides what i was looking for, and yes it is working. I must must have 
misunderstood  shomething.

Sorry!
